title: Combat & Honor
objective: Learn the core verbs of conflict and the concept of honor.

data.text: To speak Klingon is to understand battle. Many of their most common words revolve around conflict, strategy, and reputation.

data.description: To attack. In Klingon syntax, the object being attacked usually comes before the verb.
To defend. A true warrior knows when to strike and when to hold their ground.
The bat'leth. The traditional curved, double-ended sword of the Klingon people. It literally translates to 'sword of honor'.
Honor. This is the central pillar of Klingon society. To lose it is worse than death.

data.question: Which of these words means 'to attack'?
What is the literal translation of the weapon 'betleH'?

data.text: Excellent! You now have the vocabulary to describe a skirmish. Qapla'!
